Servant's Blade  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Slashing Atk Delay: 30
DMG: 11 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
DEX: +5 STA: +5 HP: +5
WT: 5.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: WAR PAL RNG SHD BRD ROG
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Skill: 1H Slashing Atk Delay: 30
DMG: 11 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.

WT: 5.0 Size: MEDIUM

Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Item Type:1H Slashing
Stackable:No
Lucy Entry By:Melany - Tunare
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 02:23:10
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 24
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

NPC Name
befallen 1.0 - Servant of T'Vyl

Befallen

this sword
QuoteReply
# Mar 07 2004 at 4:41 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591798219854
12 posts
this is a nice sword for a new guy or a new toon like my pally
▲ Page top
Which door?????????
QuoteReply
# Mar 30 2002 at 4:34 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759203743
1 post
I have a ten warrior who went in Befallen. when u first wlk in there are 4 doors. which one leads to the 3rd floor?PLZZZZ ANSWER ME, IM DESPERITE.Ps I dont like seing major twinks w/ stuff better than I.Its just not right.


Kimekose,The nameless,lvl 21
▲ Page top
RE: Which door?????????
QuoteReply
# Aug 23 2002 at 3:40 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592250388431
View User Forum
8 posts
the door to the 3rd floor is the 2nd one on the left. you need to go into the other doors..i believe the 2nd one on the right and kill the SK to get the key to open the door. Next once inside you need to be careful as you go down to the lower level..dont fall. Once you are at the bottom and have killed what you need to kill you should be in a large hall/cavern. to your left is a hallway that goes left and right. Go right. Explore this area until you find the SK. kill the SK and get the keys from him/her. I found the key to the 3rd floor on the male SK. The entrance to the 3rd floor is back the way you came where you took your right turn...take that left hallway. go right and find the stairway down to the door to 3rd floor.

Advice: at level 10 do not attempt any of this on your own..get your guild or a couple groups of people who want to go ABOVE lvl 10.

I'm a level 45 Pally on the Tarew Marr server and i took every level on my own. Then necro that died..well she died probably due to being afk or not having enough mana. They arent really known for being able to melee. I took Befallen with only a bubble of health loss.

Could I?
QuoteReply
# Aug 30 2001 at 11:25 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593966042186
10 posts
Could a 17 ranger and a 17 enchanter walk into befallen and get this blade? If so, how? Would we need to group up, or could we go invis and just go straight to the camp, kill the servant, and grab it? Can't be that easy lol...

Anyway, never been in Befallen before, so some advice would be greatly appreciated.

▲ Page top
RE: Could I?
QuoteReply
# Oct 28 2001 at 10:18 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592250348001
Scholar
153 posts
The short answer is 'no'. This sword drops from 3rd floor mobs. A full level 20 group can live down there pretty well but you won't touch the servants down there at 17. Befallen's mobs will agro you regardless of your level. I started in befallen around 13 and even at 22 I can still get overpowered in the main room if the whole room agros me. It's definately a dungeon for groups.

One thing about this sword that severely limits its value is that it is NO DROP - you can't sell it. It would only be worth about 75-100p anyway. Still, it's a decent sword with decent stats. I keep mine in the bank for corpse runs and it works well.
▲ Page top
RE: Could I?
QuoteReply
# Jan 16 2002 at 9:00 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592252810937
10 posts
regardless of your level? HA!! im level 28 and they dont even try to attack me. not even the skellies.
▲ Page top
RE: Could I?
QuoteReply
# Jan 24 2002 at 11:02 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591766526655
2 posts


Edited, Thu Jan 24 22:03:15 2002
▲ Page top
RE: Could I?
QuoteReply
# Sep 06 2002 at 2:19 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591779219070
6 posts
my level 26 de necro rips up the place solo, ghouls, elf skele, and blue mobs (very few) may aggro, but not much else. Necro apprentices will try to heal any creature you attack though, but they are weak spells so it doent matter much.
▲ Page top
Certain death at that level
QuoteReply
# Sep 04 2001 at 10:03 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592247010486
Scholar
360 posts
Not a chance. Here's why:

1.) Swarms. Everything in Befallen aggos on everyone, regardless of level. Level 35+ characters die routinely on the third floor, buried under waves of aggressive no-XP greenies that have way too many hitpoints.

2.) It's really hard to get 1-on-1 fights down there. maybe your 'chanter friend could help you. Do level 17 'chanters have AOE Mez?

3.) Escape. Getting down to the third floor is easy. Just jump down the well. Getting out is another matter entirely. You need to get through 2 locked doors - assuming here that you don't have the keys. Your 'chanter friend can just Gate out. You could get trapped (and killed) down there pretty easily.

When I went down there (I was looking for Pristine Leather but didn't find any), I was 35 (Ranger). We had a druid (to Evac us out), a cleric, and a bard along as well, similar levels. The cleric died (see Swarms, above). Then I died fighting the last of about a 7 or 8 MoB swarm. The druid and bard lived (luckily). While I was down there, a level 44 Enchantress who was camping the Primordial Ritualist bought the farm as well. We were able to get her a lvl49 Res, she gave me the next Servants Blade that dropped (she would'a given it anyway as it was totally useless to her).

Anyway - be careful. it's dangerous down there... But this is a really nice offhand for you Rangers out there. It's worth a little risk.

Arynduil Greyleaf
35th Ranger of Tunare
Norrathian Brotherhood, Erollsi Marr
▲ Page top

i'm a big sissy! to scared to go into befallen. last time i went in at 20 i was (what's the word for being taken control of?) and was running all over for a min helpless, scared the **** outta me! til luckily he ran me into the zone..phew!! it seemed like an eternity. so i swore i wouldn't go back until higher lv, but then i see all these nice drops and i want them ,so what can i do?
waaaaaa i want the nice sword waaaaaaa
▲ Page top
RE: me scared
QuoteReply
# Aug 23 2001 at 3:12 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591766406940
4 posts
Nothing to be scared about.

I go in there with my lvl 39 ranger, and everything aggs him. Everything!! With three or four casters on him, and a couple of pets, they can get me down to a bubble before I get them all killed.

Make sure your protections are as high as you can get them before venturing in there too far. If you get mobbed by more than one caster, you can't interrupt all of them.
▲ Page top

dropped on the third floor off a regular Servant of T`Vyl (gotta love having locksmith skill...keys? we done need no steenkin keys!) but still haven't found a Shimmering Ruby Stilleto, oh well guess I'll keep trying. BTW old offhand was a Crystallized Sword 11/29 and the 1 less delay in the offhand seems to be worth the 5dex/sta/hp.
▲ Page top
Comparison between icetooth claws
QuoteReply
# Jul 29 2001 at 2:07 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592248518565
113 posts
This might be a minor point but here goes:

This does have stats whereas the icetooth claws does not. However the minor stats that this gives doesn't outweigh the benefit that icetooth claws have over - weight.

This may be unique to wood elves who start out abismally low in str but at 5 stones for this, the icetooth claws look much better at 2 stones. Of course i could get rid of my singing steele and lambent but somehow I don't think so.

Nice blade
QuoteReply
# Jul 09 2001 at 10:36 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249461149
62 posts
Got one of these while camping the guy that drops the Shimmering Ruby Stilleto. The Servant was the PH and he was holding this. He was up when I came in and was a very easy kill. Ditched the Crystallized Blade to use this; even with only an 85 str, I still hit for mid-high 20s (and often with the off hand). This little gadget also got my dex over 150, happy day.

Never did get that Stilletto though...

# Jul 02 2001 at 12:38 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757705537
Scholar
57 posts
Just like the corrupted fairies and pixies in LFay and GFay, not all servers had it at the same time... some of them got it much later than the rest.
My guess is that they'll implement the new Befallen in the course of a few weeks. Watch for any server specific down times... that may be them updating new dungeons and stuff.

▲ Page top
What lvl ?
QuoteReply
# Jun 29 2001 at 11:14 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762537018
11 posts
I would love one of these for my rogues offhand, what lvl do I have to be to go there and get one ?
▲ Page top
RE: What lvl ?
QuoteReply
# Jun 30 2001 at 2:19 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762996353
119 posts
Well, Morinn, if you are at least level 15 or so, being a rogue, you could most definitely get one, but you'd have to be patient. If your Pick Lock skill was good, you might be able to open the first and third locked doors, sneaking and hiding all the way of course to avoid the mobs. Then you could just hang out on the third level and ask around and see if anyone will let you loot one. However, a couple things: it will take a while for one to drop, as in my experience I didn't see one in the two hours I was there, and be polite and courteous. Also, try not to pull any mobs if you do go down there at a low level, as I'm sure none of the high levels appreciate it.

Eitsekur, Heartbinder's Heroes
7th Hammer
▲ Page top
whats with all the no drops lately
QuoteReply
# Jun 29 2001 at 5:45 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965867885
4 posts
I play for the most part a lvl 39 ranger on Druzzil Ro i do not have the benefit of a full group most of the time i am just partnered with a friend thank god he is a druid so its rather tough to get alot of plat or items i am forced by my Job to play off peak times so not many ppl on to group with so i have to wait until i most mobs are green to get cool items lately the game and all the new REVAMPING is twards large groups of ppl i went to Befallen with my lvl 35 druid friend and almost died in the well how in the world can ppl that can use this stuff at thier lvl actually be able to get it??? wake up Verant there are ppl other than Uber guilds that would like to be able to play the game and get cool items
▲ Page top
RE: whats with all the no drops lately
QuoteReply
# Jun 29 2001 at 7:13 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592245989966
11 posts
Hey, relax...use a some punctuation once in a while.

I am glad there are NO DROP items coming out...I'm sick of dr00ds invading new zones to farm all the cool gear that us mid level players could seriously use.

I got lucky with this item...I stormed into a room, and it was camped by an Iksar monk. I settled in beside her and began binding her wounds. At this time, she informed me that there was a sword on the corpse to my left. Some players are pretty cool...but they are getting hard to find.
▲ Page top
RE: whats with all the no drops lately
QuoteReply
# Jun 29 2001 at 11:22 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Plus I think it's one way that Verant is addressing the blatant ubertwinking that's going on in the game. Nothing more depressing than seeing some smart-*** level 2 warrior with weapons better than, say, a level 50 warrior, or armor that makes a few people that go on plane raids on a regular basis sick with envy.

It is kinda depressing. My main is a 53 wiz, and i have a 39 monk twink. My monk has gear better than a lot of 50+ monks ive seen, but Ive seen the same thing with my wiz, seeing a lvl 10 wizard in better gear than i have.
